2605.22909.title: Sample-efficient benchmarking of shallow all-to-all random quantum circuits
2605.22909.summary:
- Nonlinear cross-entropy distinguishes noisy quantum computers from classical spoofers in shallow-depth circuits.
- Heavy output generation features logarithmic sample complexity at short depth.
- Analytic expressions and numerical simulations support these results for all-to-all random quantum circuits.

2605.22915.title: Unified resonant-manifold framework for dynamical quantum phase transitions
2605.22915.summary:
- A unified framework is proposed for dynamical quantum phase transitions (DQPTs).
- Manifold DQPTs are governed by resonances within the initial state manifold.
- Branch DQPTs are governed by resonances with a transitional manifold of states.

2605.22917.title: Quantum Fisher Information under decoherence with explicit wavefunctions
2605.22917.summary:
- A method to estimate quantum Fisher information under decoherence is presented.
- The method maps QFI to expectation values over a classical probability distribution.
- The framework is applied to Jastrow-Gutzwiller wave functions and various noise channels.

2605.22920.title: Estimating Green's functions with a robust quantum Arnoldi method
2605.22920.summary:
- A robust quantum Arnoldi method (ROQAM) is introduced for estimating Green's functions.
- ROQAM preserves the upper-Hessenberg structure of projected matrices through orthogonal polynomials.
- ROQAM outperforms pointwise estimation via quantum singular value transformation by multiple orders of magnitude.

2605.22922.title: Observation of associative-memory retrieval and spin-glass phases on a photonic quantum simulator
2605.22922.summary:
- Photonic quantum simulator is used to study associative memories and neural networks.
- Three distinct regimes are identified: memory retrieval, spin-glass, and paramagnetic phases.
- Experimental results confirm successful memory retrieval at low storage capacities and temperatures.

2605.22947.title: Entanglement-facilitated macroscopic cluster formation in quantum many-body dynamics
2605.22947.summary:
- Quantum systems with entanglement preserve global information.
- Entanglement suppresses fragmentation in 2D quantum Ising model.
- Initial-state entanglement stabilizes macroscopic clusters.

2605.22953.title: Chaos to Synchronization and Dissipative Quantum Scarring in Open Coupled top-Dicke model in a Lossy Cavity
2605.22953.summary:
- The open coupled-top Dicke model explores non-equilibrium phenomena in an open environment.
- Photon loss induces synchronization and coherence through a dissipation-free subspace.
- Two distinct scarring phenomena occur in the presence of dissipation, with one remaining protected and the other decaying.

2605.22966.title: Phase-dependent role of dissipation across the Aubry-Andr\'e-Harper transition
2605.22966.summary:
- Bath memory affects transport differently in extended and localized phases.
- In the extended phase, bath memory reshapes the dynamical generator.
- In the localized phase, bath memory renormalizes timescales.

2605.22977.title: Absorbing Many-Body Correlations into Core-Optimized Orbitals
2605.22977.summary:
- Researchers developed a method called Core-Optimized Orbitals (COO) to reduce the computational cost of simulating quantum many-body systems.
- COO combines a sparse Configuration Interaction (CI) wavefunction with an optimized orbital basis, significantly reducing the number of determinants required.
- The method achieves higher accuracy with fewer parameters, outperforming other methods like SU(-adapted DMRG and TrimCI.

2605.22980.title: Automatic De-Quantization of Quantum Programs Using Constant Propagation
2605.22980.summary:
- Researchers propose a method to de-quantize quantum operations using constant propagation.
- The approach, hybrid quantum-classical constant propagation, reduces quantum operations by replacing them with classical instructions.
- Results show that this method can reduce costly multi-qubit operations, making quantum programs more practical for near-term devices.

2605.22933.title: Emergent heavy-tailed distributions from a Markovian random walk
2605.22933.summary:
- A Markovian random walk exhibits heavy-tailed distributions due to a local, deterministic feedback mechanism.
- The walk's stationary state is a non-Gaussian, Lorentz-like distribution with power-law tails decaying as |x|^{-2}.
- A non-zero initial acceleration drives the walker away from the origin, enabling scale-free statistics and a normalizable distribution.

2605.23228.title: Exact solution of generalized gauge-invariant Ising chains with multi-spin interactions
2605.23228.summary:
- Exact solutions are found for generalized gauge-invariant n-chain Ising models with multi-spin interactions.
- An explicit expression for the partition function is derived using the transfer-matrix method.
- The Wilson loop behavior is analyzed, revealing area-law and perimeter-law regimes.

2605.23364.title: Inviscid scaling in the Kuramoto-Sivashinsky equation from functional renormalization group and direct numerical simulations
2605.23364.summary:
- The Kuramoto-Sivashinsky equation features a scaling regime with z=1 at intermediate scales.
- This regime arises from the vanishing of effective viscosity in the equation.
- The behavior pertains to the inviscid-Burgers universality class.

2605.23758.title: Order-Disorder Tricriticality in $\mathrm{A}_n \mathrm{B}_n$ Star Polymer Melts
2605.23758.summary:
- Tricriticality occurs in symmetric star-polymer melts due to arm number n.
- A tricritical arm number, ntc ≈ 4475, is identified.
- Transition becomes first order for nn_tc with a quadratic phase-boundary shift.

2605.23781.title: Multi-field Return Point Memory
2605.23781.summary:
- Researchers generalize partial ordering to systems with multiple control fields.
- They demonstrate return-point memory in a zero-temperature Ising model.
- Multiple fields grant precise control and complex behavior in the system.

2605.23789.title: Orientable Surfactants on Thin Liquid Films: A Dynamic Density-Functional Theory Approach
2605.23789.summary:
- Surfactants are treated as polar uniaxial particles in a dynamic density-functional theory approach.
- The approach preserves gradient dynamics form by defining a free energy with interfacial contributions.
- Thin-film equations are derived to govern film height, surfactant concentration, and polarization field.

2605.23803.title: Chirality-sensitive mobility and dissipation of Brownian motion on a helical landscape
2605.23803.summary:
- A particle's Brownian motion in a 2D helical landscape separates into free and harmonic motion under isotropic damping.
- Anisotropic damping destroys separability, leading to a linear Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process in phase space.
- The system's stationary dynamics and mobility tensor are derived, including cross-response between axial and angular motion.

2605.22906.title: Weak wave turbulence as a precursor to universal coarsening in a homogeneous Bose gas
2605.22906.summary:
- A homogeneous Bose gas exhibits universal coarsening through phase-ordering kinetics.
- Weak wave turbulence describes the initial transport of particles to low momenta.
- The momentum distribution follows a power-law with exponent γ = 4(.

2605.22982.title: Transient and asymptotic Taylor--Aris dispersion of Brownian rods in arbitrary regular-polygonal ducts
2605.22982.summary:
- Taylor-Aris dispersion of Brownian rods in non-circular ducts is influenced by pressure-driven shear and duct geometry.
- A local shear-aligned Jeffery-Brownian closure gives four transport fields for rods in regular-polygonal ducts.
- Rod alignment enhances the Taylor coefficient by reducing transverse mixing, with a small effect on mean speed.

2605.23046.title: Weakly nonlinear interaction of capillary waves in a finite system: leading interaction process and scales' range of direct energy cascade
2605.23046.summary:
- Resonant interactions dominate weakly nonlinear capillary wave interactions.
- Zakharov-Filonenko spectrum forms in isotropic capillary wave turbulence.
- Finite system limits the range of scales for the Zakharov-Filonenko spectrum.

2605.23166.title: Free surfaces in turbulence -- A unified framework from water surfaces to elastic solids
2605.23166.summary:
- A unified framework describes deformable surfaces in turbulent flows.
- The framework distinguishes between surface-driven and flow-driven dynamics.
- Numerical simulations validate the framework for air-water and rubber interfaces.

2605.23303.title: On the Applicability of the Gas-Kinetic Scheme with Kinetic Boundary Conditions for Near-Continuum Hypersonic Flows
2605.23303.summary:
- The gas-kinetic scheme (GKS) captures near-equilibrium flow physics using the Chapman-Enskog expansion.
- GKS employs kinetic boundary conditions for velocity slip and temperature jump.
- GKS provides a more physically faithful representation of non-equilibrium wall interactions than conventional CFD solvers.

2605.23309.title: Full-component reconstruction of three-dimensional fluid stress tensors
2605.23309.summary:
- Researchers developed U-FlowPET to reconstruct 3D fluid stress tensors without assumptions.
- U-FlowPET integrates photoelastic tomography with fluid mechanics governing equations.
- The method validates results using analytical, numerical, and experimental datasets.

2605.23396.title: CHIMERA: A wide Reynolds number range Taylor-Couette facility
2605.23396.summary:
- A Taylor-Couette facility (CHIMERA) is designed for wide Reynolds number range studies.
- The facility uses a torsion pendulum for torque measurement, eliminating rotating transducers.
- Measurements cover over five decades in Reynolds number, from classical to superfluid regimes.

2605.23620.title: From Optical Breakdown to Bubble Inception: A Coupled Plasma-Thermal Framework for Nanosecond Laser-Induced Cavitation in Water
2605.23620.summary:
- A coupled plasma-thermal framework unifies plasma and thermal effects in laser-induced cavitation.
- Plasma-induced thermoelastic acoustic relaxation governs bubble inception on nanosecond timescales.
- The framework accurately captures early time cavity formation and longtime bubble expansion.

2605.23800.title: A derivation of viscous thin film flow equations on curved surfaces
2605.23800.summary:
- Viscous thin film flow equations are derived for curved surfaces.
- Derivation includes gravitational, capillary, and Marangoni effects.
- Formulation emphasizes the importance of curvature gradients.

2605.23812.title: An Ensemble Variational approach for High-Dimensional Open-Loop Flow Control
2605.23812.summary:
- An ensemble-variational framework is proposed for high-dimensional flow control.
- It approximates cost-function gradients through a finite ensemble of perturbed control vectors.
- The method is effective in quasi-periodic and chaotic regimes, reducing kinetic energy fluctuations.

2605.23869.title: Soft Mobility Theory
2605.23869.summary:
- A soft mobility theory is proposed for deformable bodies in viscous flows.
- The theory yields a configuration-dependent ODE for generalized coordinates.
- It enables efficient inverse design of soft microrobots and other deformable systems.

2605.23776.title: Precipitation diffusion downscaling and application to out-of-distribution simulations with and without stratospheric aerosol injection
2605.23776.summary:
- A deep learning diffusion downscaler generates realistic 25° precipitation data.
- The downscaler accurately recreates climate change projections of extreme precipitation.
- SAI scenarios may nearly halve the CONUS-average increase in yearly max precipitation.

2605.23778.title: The physics of AI weather models
2605.23778.summary:
- AI weather models may solve physical equations differently than conventional models.
- AI models represent the atmosphere in similar ways despite architectural differences.
- AI models implement a particle description of the atmosphere, following a gradient flow.

2605.23875.title: Atmosphere as a steam engine
2605.23875.summary:
- The Earth's atmosphere operates a steam cycle with water vapor evaporating, expanding, condensing, and returning as precipitation.
- The atmospheric steam cycle generates power through expansion work, gravitational work, and a correction for incomplete condensation.
- Estimated global steam-engine power is 4 ± 9 W/m2, comparable to total atmospheric power.

2605.23403.title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Corrective Diffusion Modeling for Meteorological Downscaling
2605.23403.summary:
- A hybrid quantum-classical model improves statistical downscaling of weather fields.
- The model combines variational quantum circuits with a classical diffusion UNet for probabilistic downscaling.
- Hybrid models show improved performance and preserved spatial organization compared to classical models.

2412.00936.title: Smoothing and spatial verification of global fields
2412.00936.summary:
- Two new methodologies for smoothing global fields are presented.
- They can handle irregular grids and missing data.
- Smoothing-based metrics can now be calculated in a global domain.

2503.05331.title: Radiosonde-constrained reconstructions reveal a weakening Northern Hadley circulation
2503.05331.summary:
- Radiosonde data reveals a weakening Northern Hadley circulation since
- Climate models and radiosonde reconstructions agree on a weakening trend.
- Reanalyses systematically underestimate NHC strength, leading to a discrepancy with climate models.

2601.05841.title: Non-stationary time series attribution for heatwaves over Europe
2601.05841.summary:
- A non-stationary Markov process models temporal dependence in extreme events.
- Attribution results show strong evidence for anthropogenic climate change in Europe.
- Mean warming's effect is eliminated to examine attribution statement changes.

2605.11639.title: Enabling High-Accuracy Data Assimilation with Limited Ensembles via Machine Learning-Based Covariance Correction
2605.11639.summary:
- A machine learning-based EnKF framework is proposed to improve accuracy with small ensemble sizes.
- A multilayer perceptron predicts covariance differences between limited and large ensembles.
- The predicted differences are incorporated into the EnKF algorithm for improved analysis results.

2605.22901.title: A No-Go Theorem for the Mass-Radius Relation of Solitons
2605.22901.summary:
- A no-go theorem excludes solitons with mass-radius index Γ in the range [0, d].
- The theorem applies to real scalar field theories in d spatial dimensions.
- Typical non-topological, non-relativistic solitons cannot have Γ in the range [0, d].

2605.23001.title: Nonlinear Wave Propagation in 1D Polycatenated Ring Chains
2605.23001.summary:
- Nonlinear wave dynamics occur in 1D polycatenated ring chains due to tensile activation and internal flexibility.
- Nonlinear waves exhibit a compact leading wavefront and persistent trailing oscillations due to energy partitioning.
- The system's nonlinearity can be tuned by altering the rings' geometric aspect ratio and contact angles.

2605.23688.title: Adjoint inverse design of microresonator frequency combs
2605.23688.summary:
- Microresonator frequency combs are optimized using an adjoint-based inverse-design framework.
- The framework directly optimizes the comb spectrum with respect to pre-defined objectives.
- Inverse design enables compact on-chip light sources with tailored properties.

2605.22236.title: Beyond descendants: integrable observables for cohomological field theories
2605.22236.summary:
- Integrable observables are proposed as alternatives to Witten's psi classes in cohomological field theories.
- Integrable observables retain integrability properties and are used to recover known hierarchies and reveal new features.
- The framework yields a proof of Miura equivalence and a new proof of Witten's conjecture.
